approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognised assets and liabilities as eligible hedged items, which makes the fair value hedge model a relevant top-level hedge-accounting approach for FX risk on a recognised intragroup receivable in consolidated accounts.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies highly probable forecast transactions as hedged items and specifically permits certain forecast intragroup foreign-currency transactions in consolidated financial statements, so the cash flow hedge model is a relevant peer approach.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 and IFRS 9 recognise hedging of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge-accounting model for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.
